_meta.review_note: Jerusalem wholesale market rates. Reviewed quarterly.
_meta.reviewer: Updated by Claude.ai research — verify against moag.gov.il and Jerusalem market sources before deploying.

changelog.note: Initial structured extraction from App.tsx. Added ewe (female lamb) as separate price point.
Migrated to standalone prices.json. No price changes.

bull.source: Central Cattle Market, Moshav Beit Dagan
bull.note: Israeli cattle market average. Adult male, ~500kg.

ram.note: Domestic production keeps prices lower than comparable US animals.

lamb.note: Male yearling lamb at Israeli auction. High domestic consumption; robust supply.

ewe.note: Female lamb (כַּבְשָׂה). ~10% below male lamb due to lighter carcass weight. Used for chatat and female-specific offerings.

goat.note: Widely raised domestically. Used for communal Mussaf chataot.

bird.note: Squab/turtledove equivalent. Poverty-tier substitute for chatas and olah.

issaron_flour.source: Solet — semolina-grade wheat flour; Israeli supermarket retail (Sugat/Osem semolina)
issaron_flour.note: Solet is properly semolina-grade wheat, coarsely ground and sifted — not fine flour (Menachos 27a, Rashi s.v. Solet=Geres; Avos 5:15). Price per kg. R' Naeh baseline: ~2.4kg per issaron.

log_oil.source: Domestic Israeli extra-virgin olive oil; Galilee production
log_oil.note: Significantly cheaper than US kosher imports. Mikdash required first-pressing quality. Price per log (~0.31L by R' Naeh).

log_wine.source: Israeli table wine; Golan Heights Winery, Carmel
log_wine.note: No import markup. Must be aged 40+ days (Menachos 87a). Price per log.

frankincense.source: Machane Yehuda spice market; imported Omani frankincense
frankincense.note: Boswellia sacra resin. Komatz approx 1-2 oz per offering.

salt.note: Obligatory on all offerings. Negligible cost.

wood.note: Nine acceptable species (Tamid 29b). Nominal per-offering estimate for wood consumed on the altar.

ketores.note: Annual batch: 368 maneh (~184kg) of 11 spices. Dominant costs: saffron (16 maneh × ~NIS 50/g), onycha (70 maneh × ~NIS 4/g), stacte (70 maneh × ~NIS 3/g), plus galbanum, spikenard, myrrh, cassia, costus, cinnamon, aromatic bark, frankincense. Annual total ~NIS 782,000 ÷ 730 offerings = NIS 1,072 per offering. Saffron alone >50% of cost.
